title: Films, Videos, and Publications Classification Act 1993

sections.title: Objectionable publication
sections.content: A publication is objectionable if it describes, depicts, expresses, or otherwise deals with matters such as sex, horror, crime, cruelty, or violence in such a manner that it is likely to be injurious to the public good.

sections.title: Possession of objectionable publication
sections.content: Every person who knowingly has in their possession any objectionable publication is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years.

sections.title: Distribution of objectionable publication
sections.content: Every person who knowingly distributes or makes available any objectionable publication is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years.

sections.title: Inspection and search powers
sections.content: An Inspector of Publications or a constable may enter premises and inspect or search for publications where they have reasonable grounds to believe an offence is being committed.
